result.markdown:
--- title: "Analyzing Real-Time Sports Odds with Python" author: "Ben Ballard" date: "2026-02-18" categories: [Python, Sports Betting, Data Science, APIs] jupyter: python3 execute: freeze: true echo: true --- In [Part 1: Unlocking Sports Betting with Python](https://medium.com/@ben.g.ballard/unlocking-sports-betting-with-python), we pulled live odds from The Odds API and compared bookmaker lines in a Jupyter notebook. In this tutorial, we're going deeper. We will: - Build a **Live Odds Board** that highlights the best lines for every game. - Calculate **Implied Win Probabilities** to see what the bookmakers really think. - Analyze **Player Props** to find discrepancies between books (arbitrage/value opportunities). We'll do all of this in a standard Jupyter notebook/Python script. Let's dig in. I've pre-loaded the necessary libraries and the helper functions from Part 1. Now, let's get to the good stuff: **Fetching the Live Odds**. ## Fetching Live Odds Let's pull today's NBA odds. Same endpoint as Part 1 — we're just requesting all three market types at once: moneyline (`h2h`), spreads, and totals. ::: {#4452e4e0 .cell execution_count=2} ``` {.python .cell-code} SPORT = 'basketball_nba' url = f'https://api.the-odds-api.com/v4/sports/{SPORT}/odds/' params = { 'apiKey': API_KEY, 'regions': 'us', 'markets': 'h2h,spreads,totals', 'oddsFormat': 'american', } resp = requests.get(url, params=params) resp.raise_for_status() events = resp.json() print(f'Got {len(events)} upcoming games') print(f'Quota used: {resp.headers.get("x-requests-used", "?")} | Remaining: {resp.headers.get("x-requests-remaining", "?")}') ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Got 13 upcoming games Quota used: 197 | Remaining: 303 ``` ::: ::: ::: {#a4a4ca46 .cell execution_count=3} ``` {.python .cell-code} df = flatten_odds(events) print(f'{len(df)} rows across {df["game"].nunique()} games and {df["bookmaker"].nunique()} bookmakers') df.head() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` 594 rows across 13 games and 9 bookmakers ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=3} ```{=html}
game home_team away_team commence_time bookmaker market outcome price point
0 Atlanta Hawks @ Philadelphia 76ers Philadelphia 76ers Atlanta Hawks 2026-02-20T00:10:00Z FanDuel h2h Atlanta Hawks -104 NaN
1 Atlanta Hawks @ Philadelphia 76ers Philadelphia 76ers Atlanta Hawks 2026-02-20T00:10:00Z FanDuel h2h Philadelphia 76ers -112 NaN
2 Atlanta Hawks @ Philadelphia 76ers Philadelphia 76ers Atlanta Hawks 2026-02-20T00:10:00Z FanDuel spreads Atlanta Hawks -114 1.5
3 Atlanta Hawks @ Philadelphia 76ers Philadelphia 76ers Atlanta Hawks 2026-02-20T00:10:00Z FanDuel spreads Philadelphia 76ers -106 -1.5
4 Atlanta Hawks @ Philadelphia 76ers Philadelphia 76ers Atlanta Hawks 2026-02-20T00:10:00Z FanDuel totals Over -108 240.5
``` ::: ::: ## Section 1: Live Odds Table First, let's look at the moneyline odds. We'll pivot the data so each bookmaker gets its own column, then highlight the best odds in green using Pandas styling. ::: {#23e88d10 .cell execution_count=4} ``` {.python .cell-code} # Filter to moneyline odds only h2h = df[df['market'] == 'h2h'].copy() # Pivot: game + team as rows, bookmakers as columns pivot = h2h.pivot_table( index=['game', 'outcome'], columns='bookmaker', values='price', aggfunc='first', ) # Build a Plotly table with best odds highlighted in green books = pivot.columns.tolist() row_labels = [f"{game} — {team}" for game, team in pivot.index] cell_values = [] cell_colors = [] for col in books: col_vals, col_colors = [], [] for (game, team), row in pivot.iterrows(): val = row[col] if pd.notna(val): col_vals.append(f"{val:.0f}") col_colors.append('#2ecc71' if val == row.max() else 'white') else: col_vals.append('-') col_colors.append('white') cell_values.append(col_vals) cell_colors.append(col_colors) fig = go.Figure(data=[go.Table( header=dict( values=['Game / Team'] + books, fill_color='#34495e', font=dict(color='white', size=12), align='left', ), cells=dict( values=[row_labels] + cell_values, fill_color=[['#f8f9fa'] * len(row_labels)] + cell_colors, align='left', font=dict(size=11), ) )]) fig.update_layout( height=max(300, len(row_labels) * 35 + 80), margin=dict(l=0, r=0, t=20, b=0), ) fig.show() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: See how some bookmakers offer slightly better odds? Those differences matter. Let's make it even easier to spot the best lines. ## Section 2: Best Line Finder This one's my favorite. For each team in each game, we find which bookmaker has the best moneyline and how much of an "edge" it has over the second-best option. ::: {#78b9eef2 .cell execution_count=5} ``` {.python .cell-code} best_lines = [] for (game, outcome), row in pivot.iterrows(): best_book = row.idxmax() best_price = row.max() second_best = row.nlargest(2).iloc[-1] if len(row.dropna()) > 1 else best_price best_lines.append({ 'Game': game, 'Team': outcome, 'Best Book': best_book, 'Best Odds': int(best_price) if pd.notna(best_price) else None, '2nd Best': int(second_best) if pd.notna(second_best) else None, 'Edge': int(best_price - second_best) if pd.notna(best_price) and pd.notna(second_best) else 0, }) best_df = pd.DataFrame(best_lines) best_df.sort_values('Edge', ascending=False) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=5} ```{=html}
Game Team Best Book Best Odds 2nd Best Edge
0 Atlanta Hawks @ Philadelphia 76ers Atlanta Hawks Bovada 100 -103 203
21 Phoenix Suns @ San Antonio Spurs San Antonio Spurs BetUS -260 -275 15
6 Dallas Mavericks @ Minnesota Timberwolves Dallas Mavericks BetRivers 450 440 10
7 Dallas Mavericks @ Minnesota Timberwolves Minnesota Timberwolves FanDuel -590 -600 10
20 Phoenix Suns @ San Antonio Spurs Phoenix Suns BetRivers 250 240 10
4 Brooklyn Nets @ Cleveland Cavaliers Brooklyn Nets LowVig.ag 708 700 8
19 Orlando Magic @ Sacramento Kings Sacramento Kings BetMGM 300 293 7
16 Milwaukee Bucks @ New Orleans Pelicans Milwaukee Bucks FanDuel 142 136 6
22 Toronto Raptors @ Chicago Bulls Chicago Bulls Bovada 200 195 5
10 Detroit Pistons @ New York Knicks Detroit Pistons DraftKings 145 140 5
18 Orlando Magic @ Sacramento Kings Orlando Magic LowVig.ag -345 -350 5
8 Denver Nuggets @ Los Angeles Clippers Denver Nuggets Bovada -180 -184 4
17 Milwaukee Bucks @ New Orleans Pelicans New Orleans Pelicans DraftKings -162 -165 3
24 Utah Jazz @ Memphis Grizzlies Memphis Grizzlies DraftKings -170 -172 2
25 Utah Jazz @ Memphis Grizzlies Utah Jazz FanDuel 144 142 2
14 Indiana Pacers @ Washington Wizards Indiana Pacers FanDuel -126 -128 2
1 Atlanta Hawks @ Philadelphia 76ers Philadelphia 76ers FanDuel -112 -113 1
12 Houston Rockets @ Charlotte Hornets Charlotte Hornets FanDuel 156 155 1
9 Denver Nuggets @ Los Angeles Clippers Los Angeles Clippers LowVig.ag 162 161 1
3 Boston Celtics @ Golden State Warriors Golden State Warriors MyBookie.ag 191 190 1
23 Toronto Raptors @ Chicago Bulls Toronto Raptors LowVig.ag -219 -220 1
2 Boston Celtics @ Golden State Warriors Boston Celtics LowVig.ag -214 -215 1
15 Indiana Pacers @ Washington Wizards Washington Wizards BetRivers 112 112 0
11 Detroit Pistons @ New York Knicks New York Knicks BetOnline.ag -155 -155 0
5 Brooklyn Nets @ Cleveland Cavaliers Cleveland Cavaliers BetRivers -1000 -1000 0
13 Houston Rockets @ Charlotte Hornets Houston Rockets BetOnline.ag -175 -175 0
``` ::: ::: The "Edge" column shows the difference in odds between the best and second-best bookmaker. Higher edge = more value shopping around. In the dashboard, this becomes a sortable table so you can instantly spot the biggest disagreements. ## Section 3: Implied Probability Chart American odds (like -150 or +130) are hard to read at a glance. Let's convert everything to **implied probabilities** and visualize them. This tells us the "true" win probability the bookmaker is assigning to each team. ::: {#0f9051c4 .cell execution_count=6} ``` {.python .cell-code} # Average odds across bookmakers for each team (consensus line) consensus = h2h.groupby(['game', 'outcome'])['price'].mean().reset_index() consensus['implied_prob'] = consensus['price'].apply(american_to_implied_prob) consensus['prob_pct'] = (consensus['implied_prob'] * 100).round(1) consensus[['game', 'outcome', 'price', 'prob_pct']].head() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=6} ```{=html}
game outcome price prob_pct
0 Atlanta Hawks @ Philadelphia 76ers Atlanta Hawks -82.000000 45.1
1 Atlanta Hawks @ Philadelphia 76ers Philadelphia 76ers -115.555556 53.6
2 Boston Celtics @ Golden State Warriors Boston Celtics -225.222222 69.3
3 Boston Celtics @ Golden State Warriors Golden State Warriors 186.333333 34.9
4 Brooklyn Nets @ Cleveland Cavaliers Brooklyn Nets 672.666667 12.9
``` ::: ::: ::: {#2f85b63d .cell execution_count=7} ``` {.python .cell-code} # Build the stacked bar chart games = consensus['game'].unique() fig = go.Figure() for game in games: game_data = consensus[consensus['game'] == game] for _, row in game_data.iterrows(): prob = row['prob_pct'] fig.add_trace(go.Bar( y=[game], x=[prob], name=row['outcome'], orientation='h', text=f"{row['outcome']}: {prob:.1f}%", textposition='inside', showlegend=False, marker_color='#2ecc71' if prob >= 50 else '#e74c3c', )) fig.update_layout( barmode='stack', xaxis_title='Implied Probability (%)', yaxis_title='', height=max(400, len(games) * 60), title='Implied Win Probabilities (Consensus Odds)', ) fig.show() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: Green = favorite, red = underdog. You can hover over each bar (if running locally) to see the exact probability. ## Section 4: Player Props Explorer This is where it gets really fun. We'll pick a game, pull player props (points, rebounds, assists), and see where bookmakers disagree on the lines. Bigger disagreements = potential value bets. Let's grab the first game and see what props are available. ::: {#5ede69fd .cell execution_count=8} ``` {.python .cell-code} # Pick the first game event_id = events[0]['id'] game_name = f"{events[0]['away_team']} @ {events[0]['home_team']}" print(f'Fetching props for: {game_name}') props_url = f'https://api.the-odds-api.com/v4/sports/{SPORT}/events/{event_id}/odds/' props_params = { 'apiKey': API_KEY, 'regions': 'us', 'markets': 'player_points,player_rebounds,player_assists', 'oddsFormat': 'american', } props_resp = requests.get(props_url, params=props_params) props_resp.raise_for_status() props_data = props_resp.json() print(f'Quota used: {props_resp.headers.get("x-requests-used", "?")} | Remaining: {props_resp.headers.get("x-requests-remaining", "?")}') ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Fetching props for: Atlanta Hawks @ Philadelphia 76ers Quota used: 200 | Remaining: 300 ``` ::: ::: ::: {#d8f7e11c .cell execution_count=9} ``` {.python .cell-code} props_df = flatten_props(props_data) print(f'{len(props_df)} prop lines across {props_df["player"].nunique()} players') props_df.head() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` 732 prop lines across 17 players ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=9} ```{=html}
bookmaker market player side line price
0 FanDuel player_assists Jalen Johnson Over 8.5 -102
1 FanDuel player_assists Jalen Johnson Under 8.5 -125
2 FanDuel player_assists Trendon Watford Over 3.5 144
3 FanDuel player_assists Trendon Watford Under 3.5 -186
4 FanDuel player_assists Onyeka Okongwu Over 2.5 -120
``` ::: ::: ::: {#6e310ba6 .cell execution_count=10} ``` {.python .cell-code} # Scatter plot: points props, Over side only points = props_df[(props_df['market'] == 'player_points') & (props_df['side'] == 'Over')].copy() if not points.empty: fig = px.scatter( points, x='line', y='price', color='bookmaker', hover_data=['player', 'bookmaker', 'line', 'price'], text='player', title=f'Points Props — {game_name}', ) fig.update_traces(textposition='top center', textfont_size=9) fig.update_layout( xaxis_title='Points Line', yaxis_title='Odds (American)', height=600, ) fig.show() else: print('No points props available for this game yet.') ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: When you see the same player at the same line but with different odds across bookmakers — that's where line shopping pays off. The scatter plot makes it visual: clusters of dots at the same x-position (line) with spread along the y-axis (odds) means bookmakers disagree. ## What's Next? We've now built a script that: * Pulls live odds * Finds the best lines * Calculates win probabilities * Scans for player prop disparities But there's one problem: Running a script manually every time you want to check odds is annoying. In the next article, we're going to take this code and wrap it into a **Streamlit Dashboard**. We'll build a live web app that: - Runs 24/7 - Auto-refreshes every 5 minutes - Visualizes this data in a clean, interactive UI - Can be deployed to the web (so you can check it from your phone) Stay tuned for the next post.
